headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,710, SILVER ENDS WEEK BELOW $76 AS MARKETS BRACE FOR LATE-2026 RATE CUTS
snippet: Gold traded near $4,710 per ounce on April 25 while silver closed the week below $76/oz, both metals posting weekly declines of roughly 2% and 4% respectively amid limited progress in geopolitical peace negotiations. Gold is up 41.85% year-over-year and silver has climbed more than 150% over the past year. A Reuters poll published this week found the Federal Reserve's first rate cut has been pushed back to late 2026 on war-driven inflation risks — a macro headwind for near-term precious metals sentiment.

headline: TECK DOUBLES EBITDA TO C$2.1B ON RECORD COPPER SALES — Q1 2026 BLOWOUT
snippet: Teck Resources posted Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA of C$2.09 billion, up 125% year-over-year, driven by record copper sales volumes and prices averaging US$5.83/lb at its QB operations. Revenue surged to C$3.94 billion from C$2.29 billion a year earlier, beating analyst estimates by 25%, with EPS of $1.75 versus a $1.17 consensus. Copper sales volumes of 70,300 tonnes were a quarterly record as inventory built during the ramp-up was drawn down.

headline: UR-ENERGY FLIPS THE SWITCH AT SHIRLEY BASIN: SECOND U.S. URANIUM ISR MINE NOW ONLINE
snippet: Ur-Energy (NYSE: URG) commenced in-situ recovery uranium mining at its Shirley Basin project in Carbon County, Wyoming on April 23, marking its second active U.S. uranium mine and advancing domestic production capacity. The project carries licensed capacity of 2.0 million lb U₃O₈ per year and approximately 9.1 million lb in measured-and-indicated resources over a nine-year mine life. Combined with Lost Creek, Ur-Energy's total annual licensed production capacity now stands at 4.2 million lb U₃O₈.

headline: CONTANGO SILVER & GOLD MAPS OUT $63M EXPLORATION BLITZ ACROSS ALASKA AND B.C.
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. has outlined a $63 million exploration budget for 2026 across its consolidated portfolio of advanced gold and critical metals projects in Alaska and Northern British Columbia. The program follows the company's recently completed merger with Dolly Varden Silver Corporation and represents one of the larger junior-focused exploration commitments in the Pacific Northwest corridor this year. Management framed the budget as reflecting strong confidence in the project pipeline under elevated gold and silver prices.

headline: IVANHOE'S PLATREEF SHAFT #3 NEARS COMPLETION — HOISTING CAPACITY TO JUMP FIVEFOLD
snippet: Ivanhoe Mines (TSX: IVN) announced on April 23 that Shaft #3 at its Platreef platinum-palladium-nickel-gold project in South Africa is on track to be ready-to-hoist in early Q2 2026, increasing total hoisting capacity from 0.8 Mtpa to approximately 5.0 Mtpa — a roughly fivefold increase. Underground loading box construction was recently completed, with the permanent headframe installation and conveyor infrastructure on track for Q1 close-out. Once the shaft is hoisting production ore, the concentrator is expected to reach 80% of nameplate capacity by mid-year.

headline: CRITICAL METALS LOCKS IN $120M U.S. BACKING FOR GREENLAND RARE EARTH GIANT TANBREEZ
snippet: Critical Metals Corp. secured a $120 million Letter of Intent from the U.S. Export-Import Bank to support development financing for its Tanbreez rare earth project in Greenland, one of the world's largest undeveloped rare earth deposits. The deal cements Western access to a strategic asset as the Trump administration rolls out Project Vault — $10 billion in EXIM financing and $2 billion in private capital for a U.S. Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve. Greenland has become a flashpoint for rare earth geopolitics as the U.S. and allies race to build supply chains independent of China.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE LOCKS UP C$3.8B FINLAND GOLD CONSOLIDATION — THREE DEALS, ONE GREENSTONE BELT
snippet: Agnico Eagle (TSX/NYSE: AEM) announced April 20 a three-part consolidation of Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt, anchored by a C$2.9 billion acquisition of Rupert Resources and its Ikkari gold project — located just 50 km from Agnico's existing Kittila mine. The company will also acquire Aurion Resources for ~$481 million and B2Gold's 70% stake in Fingold Ventures for $444 million, bringing the combined outlay to roughly C$3.8 billion. Agnico projects up to US$500 million in synergies from integrating Ikkari into its Finland operating platform.

headline: USA RARE EARTH BETS $2.8B ON BRAZIL'S SERRA VERDE TO BUILD SUPPLY CHAIN OUTSIDE CHINA
snippet: USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR) announced April 20 it will acquire Brazilian rare earth miner Serra Verde Group in a $2.8 billion cash-and-stock transaction — $300 million cash plus 126.8 million newly issued shares — adding a producing rare earth mine and processing plant in Brazil. The deal extends USA Rare Earth's mine-to-magnet supply chain outside Asia, directly targeting Western dependence on Chinese rare earth processing. The stock surged 13% on the announcement and is up approximately 68% year-to-date.

headline: LITHIUM PRICES NEARLY DOUBLED IN Q1 2026 AS CHINA'S VAT REBATE ERA WINDS DOWN
snippet: Lithium carbonate prices roughly doubled during Q1 2026, climbing from ~US$13,433/t in early December to over US$26,000/t, as Beijing's phased rollback of export VAT rebates on lithium-ion batteries and materials drove an export rush before the April 1 rate cut from 9% to 6%. Full elimination of the rebate is set for January 1, 2027, erasing the 'China discount' that had suppressed global lithium pricing for two years. Additional supply tightness stems from planned permit cancellations in China's Jiangxi province and the extended suspension of CATL's Jianxiawo mine.

headline: PALADIN ENERGY LIFTS URANIUM OUTPUT GUIDANCE AS LANGER HEINRICH RAMP ACCELERATES IN Q3
snippet: Paladin Energy (ASX/TSX: PDN) raised its FY2026 uranium production guidance to 4.5–4.8 million lb U₃O₈ from a prior range of 4.0–4.4 million lb, after Q3 output of 1.29 million lb at Langer Heinrich in Namibia came in 5% above the prior quarter with a 92% plant recovery rate. The company completed full commissioning of its mining fleet during the quarter and expects the ramp phase to conclude by end of FY2026, with full nameplate production targeted for FY2027. Paladin's stock has risen approximately 186% over the past year as the Langer Heinrich restart gains credibility.

headline: COT REPORT: GOLD AND SILVER SPECS HOLD HISTORICALLY LARGE LONGS HEADING INTO WEEK-END
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Commitment of Traders (COT) analysis for gold, silver, and the U.S. dollar index — published April 24 — covers speculator positioning following a volatile week that saw gold trade in a wide range and silver suffer a near 7% weekly decline before recovering. The COT report is a key sentiment indicator for junior mining investors, tracking managed money net long/short positioning against commercial hedger activity. Large speculator net long positioning in gold remains historically elevated, which some analysts read as a potential contrarian headwind.

headline: ASIAN METALS UPDATE: COPPER RETREATS BELOW $6, STRAIT OF HORMUZ CLOSURE ADDS COST PRESSURE
snippet: GoldSeek's April 24 Asian markets update flags copper retreating below $6/lb on investor caution over U.S.-Iran peace negotiations, though Chinese restocking activity ahead of the May 1–5 Labour Day holiday cushioned the downside. The continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz is driving diesel and energy costs higher — a direct headwind for mining operating margins given diesel represents 15–20% of cash expenses at most producers. Chinese smelters produced a record 1.33 million tonnes of refined copper in March, the highest since records began in 1990, adding to near-term oversupply concerns.
